title: The Ned Liest Catch

jokes.text: We're all bursting with pride after our girls' basketball team beat Shelbyville two-nothing in triple overtime! Now let's make some noise. Using our... inside voices!
jokes.setup: Excited announcement about a basketball victory calling for celebration
jokes.punchline: The contradiction of making noise with inside voices
jokes.explanation: Classic Skinner bureaucratic overthinking, perfectly in character. The buildup to anticlimax is well-structured.

jokes.text: I'm sorry, but the team all got food poisoning at the pre-rally egg salad sandwich party. Luckily, as equipment manager, I... was not... invited!
jokes.setup: Apologetic explanation about food poisoning
jokes.punchline: The bitter irony of being lucky to be excluded
jokes.explanation: The pause before 'invited' sells the social rejection perfectly. Economy is excellent.

jokes.text: Let chaos reign!
jokes.setup: Free play is announced due to assembly cancellation
jokes.punchline: Bart's over-the-top revolutionary declaration for playground time
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bart line - maximum drama for minimal stakes. Two words that capture his anarchic spirit.

jokes.text: No one cancels my free play! / It's okay. Thanks for making me part of the revolution.
jokes.setup: Bart's outrage at cancelled free play
jokes.punchline: Martin's polite gratitude for being included in rebellion
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character contrast - Martin's earnest politeness during chaos. Well-earned through character consistency.

jokes.text: He's uncontrollable! Pumpkin stickers mean nothing to him!
jokes.setup: Teacher describing Bart's disruptive behavior
jokes.punchline: The absurdly mild punishment that has no effect
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation - from 'uncontrollable' to the hilariously specific pumpkin stickers. Perfect satire of ineffective school discipline.

jokes.text: Me bleachers have been weaponized!
jokes.setup: Chaos in the gymnasium
jokes.punchline: Willie's dramatic Scottish accent applied to bleachers becoming weapons
jokes.explanation: Classic Willie - treating mundane school property like military equipment. The accent sells it.

jokes.text: It was an open hand. Does that help?
jokes.setup: Edna has just slapped a student
jokes.punchline: Her attempt to minimize the assault by noting technique
jokes.explanation: Dark humor delivered with perfect deadpan. The legalistic parsing of assault is both horrifying and funny.

jokes.text: we can tolerate mild alcoholism, leaving melted cheese in the microwave, even selling A's for cigarettes. But in laying a hand on a student, you have crossed a line.
jokes.setup: Chalmers explaining what the school can tolerate
jokes.punchline: The increasingly absurd list of acceptable behavior vs. the line drawn at hitting students
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation comedy - mild alcoholism to cheese to bribery, then suddenly moral clarity. Great satire of institutional priorities.

jokes.text: Just leave the crust on their sandwiches. They'll get the message.
jokes.setup: Discussion of appropriate punishment for children
jokes.punchline: Marge's hilariously mild alternative discipline method
jokes.explanation: Perfectly captures Marge's gentle parenting style. The absurd mildness of crust-leaving as punishment is brilliant.

jokes.text: we have a zero-tolerance policy for this sort of thing when it occurs in front of witnesses.
jokes.setup: Official statement about zero tolerance
jokes.punchline: The caveat that it only applies when witnessed
jokes.explanation: Dark satire of institutional corruption. The qualifier completely undermines the stated policy.

jokes.text: Edna, you're suspended with full pay until the hearing, which is currently scheduled for 57 years from tomorrow. It was going to be 60, but I moved it up because I... I like the way you torment Skinner.
jokes.setup: Announcement of suspension hearing date
jokes.punchline: The ridiculously long timeframe and arbitrary reduction for petty reasons
jokes.explanation: Brilliant bureaucratic absurdity. The specific numbers and petty favoritism make it feel real while being impossible.

jokes.text: So I-I still get my check, but I get to stay at home? I'm afraid it's not quite that simple.
jokes.setup: Edna's hopeful interpretation of suspension
jokes.punchline: The crushing of her hopes with bureaucratic complexity
jokes.explanation: Standard setup/payoff structure. Works but not particularly memorable or quotable.

jokes.text: an educational limbo, as it were, where you are the unbaptized dead by. They never seem to like that analogy.
jokes.setup: Explaining the holding facility concept
jokes.punchline: The overly literary/religious reference and self-aware commentary
jokes.explanation: Smart literary reference with meta commentary. Chalmers' pretentiousness combined with self-awareness works well.

jokes.text: No Wi-Fi, and you may not refer to this experience as either Kafkaesque or Orwellian.
jokes.setup: Listing restrictions of the rubber room
jokes.punchline: Prohibiting accurate literary descriptions of the situation
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-joke. Prohibiting the exact words that describe the absurd situation is perfectly Simpsons.

jokes.text: The only thing I'd like more is if I could quit. / Well, I'm not quitting. I'm staying here till you give me my job back.
jokes.setup: Guard expressing desire for teachers to quit
jokes.punchline: Edna's defiant response that guarantees mutual suffering
jokes.explanation: Good character moment for Edna's stubbornness. The mutual trap they create is satisfying.

jokes.text: You'll find ways to pass the time. Me, I watch that crack in the ceiling. Hey, hey, find your own crack!
jokes.setup: Advice about passing time in the rubber room
jokes.punchline: Territorial behavior over a crack in the ceiling
jokes.explanation: Solid cringe comedy showing how institutional limbo breaks people down. The possessiveness is both sad and funny.

jokes.text: Is that ice? Uh, no, no, they're spider eggs. He's a science teacher. He'll know what to do.
jokes.setup: Question about ice formation
jokes.punchline: The horrifying truth and casual dismissal of responsibility
jokes.explanation: Dark humor with great misdirection. The casual passing of a spider egg problem is absurdly bureaucratic.

jokes.text: You shouldn't suffer for my bad deed. It should just go unpunished forever.
jokes.setup: Bart expressing remorse for getting Edna in trouble
jokes.punchline: His alternative solution that reveals his true nature
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bart logic - appearing noble while actually being completely selfish. The misdirection is expertly executed.

jokes.text: Check this out-- a dead possum. Ay, caramba!
jokes.setup: Bart finding something to show
jokes.punchline: His casual presentation of roadkill as entertainment
jokes.explanation: Classic Bart - finding gross things fascinating. The economy is good but the concept is fairly basic.

jokes.text: I'm a teacher in a bathroom with a student. That's why most of these people are here in the first place.
jokes.setup: Edna noting the inappropriate meeting location
jokes.punchline: The implication about why other teachers are in the rubber room
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about inappropriate teacher conduct. Works well but treads familiar territory.

jokes.text: No, but that's what I told everyone I did to earn the respect of the gangs.
jokes.setup: Discussion of what Edna did to Bart
jokes.punchline: Her fabricated story to gain street cred among criminals
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdity - middle-aged teacher trying to gain gang respect through exaggerated violence. Great character moment.

jokes.text: You made a dummy of me? Mm, I use it to workshop my pranks.
jokes.setup: Discovery of a dummy made to look like Edna
jokes.punchline: Bart's casual explanation of its practical purpose
jokes.explanation: Good Bart moment showing his dedication to pranking. The casual 'workshopping' language is funny.

jokes.text: I can't believe a middle-aged woman is scared of a three-story drop. / I'll show you who's middle-aged!
jokes.setup: Bart challenging Edna about fear of heights
jokes.punchline: Edna taking offense at 'middle-aged' rather than the dangerous drop
jokes.explanation: Perfect misdirection - vanity trumps self-preservation. Great character insight about priorities.

jokes.text: The one day I put cream cheese on my bagel!
jokes.setup: Ned getting hit by Edna falling from window
jokes.punchline: His concern about food waste over being hit by a person
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ned - focusing on minor details while ignoring major problems. Very character-specific.

jokes.text: Did a volcano erupt in Candyland? 'Cause I just caught me a flying red hot!
jokes.setup: Ned trying to flirt after being hit by Edna
jokes.punchline: His incredibly wholesome and awkward pickup line
jokes.explanation: Hilariously innocent flirting from Ned. The Candyland reference is perfectly character-appropriate.

jokes.text: so I don't commit the deadliest of sins-- omission-- here's my card.
jokes.setup: Ned introducing himself properly
jokes.punchline: Treating failure to exchange business cards as a deadly sin
jokes.explanation: Great Ned line mixing religious anxiety with social conventions. Very character-specific humor.

jokes.text: Good luck finding a rhythm for that
jokes.setup: Learning Edna's last name is Krabappel
jokes.punchline: Acknowledgment of the name's inherent awkwardness
jokes.explanation: Mild meta-humor about character names. Functional but not particularly memorable.

jokes.text: when they made him, they did not skimp on the puppy dog tails.
jokes.setup: Discussing Bart's nature
jokes.punchline: Creative reference to the nursery rhyme about what boys are made of
jokes.explanation: Clever nursery rhyme reference. Shows Edna's literary background while describing Bart.

jokes.text: Yeah, he's the peanut in your popcorn. Lots of fun... I guess.
jokes.setup: Discussion of Homer's character
jokes.punchline: Backhanded compliment comparing him to an unexpected snack find
jokes.explanation: Creative metaphor for Homer's disruptive nature. The hesitation sells the ambivalence.

jokes.text: I'm afraid my Maudie's putting plastic on the clouds now.
jokes.setup: Asked about his wife
jokes.punchline: Ned's euphemistic way of saying his wife is dead
jokes.explanation: Beautiful euphemism that's both touching and funny. Perfectly captures Ned's wholesome way of dealing with tragedy.

jokes.text: Probably between the hooters of a coat check girl in Shelbyville. Ha!
jokes.setup: Asked where her ex-husband is
jokes.punchline: Crude but creative description of his likely whereabouts
jokes.explanation: Good contrast with Ned's euphemism. Edna's bitterness comes through with humor.

jokes.text: It's a lot less racy than its name would lead you to believe. Crossing it off my do-not-go list.
jokes.setup: Discussing Pinkberry frozen yogurt
jokes.punchline: Ned's assumption that the name was inappropriate and his relief
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ned paranoia about innocent things. The do-not-go list is a great character detail.

jokes.text: Well, sir, I guess I better refenestrate ya.
jokes.setup: Ned saying goodbye at the window
jokes.punchline: Using 'refenestrate' (throw out window) when he means the opposite
jokes.explanation: Brilliant wordplay - using 'defenestrate' in reverse. Shows Ned's education and awkwardness perfectly.

jokes.text: You know, I am a fallen woman. / Whoa, slow down. I never go to second entendre on the first date.
jokes.setup: Edna describing herself as fallen
jokes.punchline: Ned misunderstanding and making a pun about sexual innuendo
jokes.explanation: Excellent wordplay on 'double entendre' that perfectly captures Ned's innocence and wit. Highly quotable.

jokes.text: You've been in there for three hours! What are you doing, taking a bath?!
jokes.setup: Homer waiting outside the bathroom
jokes.punchline: His practical but inappropriate question
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer obliviousness. Basic but character-appropriate.

jokes.text: Good news, Edna. The governor crushed the teachers' union, so you don't have to stay here anymore. Now I can just fire you.
jokes.setup: Chalmers announcing good news
jokes.punchline: The 'good news' is actually worse for Edna
jokes.explanation: Dark political satire. The misdirection from good news to bad news is well-executed.

jokes.text: Well, I would get a chance to see lots of my former students.
jokes.setup: Offer to work as prison guard
jokes.punchline: Edna's darkly optimistic view of reconnecting with students in prison
jokes.explanation: Dark teacher humor - finding the silver lining in student failure. Perfectly captures Edna's cynicism.

jokes.text: Oh, yes, they were. We deliberately chose a Dr. Stork so we could say it without lying.
jokes.setup: Edna noting his boys weren't delivered by stork
jokes.punchline: Ned's literal interpretation allowing him to maintain the fiction
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurd logic that's perfectly Ned. The loophole mentality for maintaining innocence is hilarious.

jokes.text: Like a mild cheddar, I get tangy when I'm sitting on the shelf.
jokes.setup: Ned explaining why waiting is worth it
jokes.punchline: Comparing himself to aging cheese
jokes.explanation: Wholesome and awkward flirting that's very Ned. The cheese metaphor is both innocent and slightly sexual.

jokes.text: Well, a little nibble won't hurt your appetite.
jokes.setup: Edna asking if she can nibble the cheese (metaphorically)
jokes.punchline: Ned continuing the metaphor while allowing minor intimacy
jokes.explanation: Continues the cheese metaphor nicely. Good callback but not particularly memorable on its own.

jokes.text: This is the best DVD about cheese-making I've ever seen.
jokes.setup: Romantic evening activity
jokes.punchline: Edna's sarcastic review of their documentary viewing
jokes.explanation: Mild sarcasm about their boring date activity. Functional but not exceptional.

jokes.text: I know it's impolite to make a move till episode 25 of a Ken Burns documentary, but you're that special.
jokes.setup: Ned making a romantic advance
jokes.punchline: His absurdly specific etiquette rule about documentary timing
jokes.explanation: Brilliant specific cultural reference. The idea of having etiquette rules for Ken Burns documentaries is perfectly Ned.

jokes.text: That's right, I'm down to my wife-blesser.
jokes.setup: Ned getting romantic
jokes.punchline: His euphemistic term for underwear
jokes.explanation: Hilariously innocent euphemism that only Ned would use. Perfect economy and character integration.

jokes.text: Snow days, strike days, half-day afternoons, and all summer long.
jokes.setup: Bart asking if she'll be around on snow days
jokes.punchline: Edna's comprehensive list of when she'll be tormenting him
jokes.explanation: Good escalating list that builds the horror for Bart. Well-structured teacher revenge.

jokes.text: Yeah. Um, he hates how learning has taken me to a place he'll never go.
jokes.setup: Explaining why his dog eats homework
jokes.punchline: Psychological analysis of dog's resentment toward education
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdity - giving the classic excuse psychological depth. Very creative and funny.

jokes.text: Little help? Little help? / Homer, could you stop throwin' balls over my fence?
jokes.setup: Homer repeatedly asking for help retrieving balls
jokes.punchline: Ned's reasonable request to stop the behavior causing the problem
jokes.explanation: Classic neighbor dynamic. Works as character comedy but not particularly innovative.

jokes.text: Don't you humiliate me in front of my kid. / I thought we had a deal, man.
jokes.setup: Ned struggling with a jack-in-the-box toy
jokes.punchline: Treating the toy as if it's intentionally defying him
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy concept. Ned's relationship with inanimate objects is funny.

jokes.text: Well, if it isn't the local school marm and his new girlfriend. / That's a burn on you, Flanders.
jokes.setup: Homer attempting an insult
jokes.punchline: His need to explain that his failed insult was meant to be insulting
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer - failing at an insult then needing to announce it was meant as one. Good character moment.

jokes.text: Whoa. Flanders, this chick does not get our thing. / Actually, she sort of does.
jokes.setup: Homer claiming Edna doesn't understand their relationship
jokes.punchline: Ned's quiet admission that she actually does
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Ned's growing awareness of Homer's treatment

jokes.text: Homer, do you know how lucky you are? Ned is a wonderful, caring man, and a darn good neighbor. / Language.
jokes.setup: Edna praising Ned
jokes.punchline: Ned objecting to the mild profanity 'darn'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ned response to incredibly mild language. Shows his extreme propriety perfectly.

jokes.text: Don't do that, Homie. It rubs off your side hair.
jokes.setup: Homer getting agitated
jokes.punchline: Marge's practical concern about his hair maintenance
jokes.explanation: Good visual reference to Homer's hair situation. Classic Marge practicality.

jokes.text: We're natural enemies, like George Washington and Abraham Lincoln.
jokes.setup: Explaining why teachers and students don't get along
jokes.punchline: Historically inaccurate comparison of presidents who never met
jokes.explanation: Good Bart logic failure. The historical impossibility makes it funnier.

jokes.text: If fairy tales have taught us one thing, it's that first wives are perfect, second wives are horrible. Just the opposite of real life.
jokes.setup: Homer explaining fairy tale logic
jokes.punchline: The cynical real-world reversal
jokes.explanation: Smart observation about fairy tale vs. reality tropes. Good setup/payoff structure.

jokes.text: Last time I did, someone slipped me a Mickey and wrote 'Churchy Joe' on my face.
jokes.setup: Ned explaining why he doesn't want to go to Moe's
jokes.punchline: His innocent experience being vandalized with a religious nickname
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ned victimization story. 'Churchy Joe' is a great nickname that captures his reputation.

jokes.text: Ned, if it's boneheaded to talk you out of seeing Edna, then I don't wanna be brainheaded.
jokes.setup: Homer trying to construct a logical argument
jokes.punchline: His nonsensical word construction and logic
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer malapropism. The made-up word 'brainheaded' is appropriately ridiculous.

jokes.text: Then I remember what it was like to have a sweet woman of my own lying in a twin bed across the hall from mine.
jokes.setup: Ned describing his marriage to Maude
jokes.punchline: The revelation of separate twin beds
jokes.explanation: Good character detail that explains Ned's sexual awkwardness. The separate beds are very period-appropriate.

jokes.text: And like any man, I put on my secret Christian underpants one leg at a time.
jokes.setup: Ned asserting his masculinity
jokes.punchline: The specific religious underwear detail
jokes.explanation: Brilliant subversion of a common expression with character-specific absurdity. Very quotable.

jokes.text: My e-mail address is Edna-lover-one-seventy-two. It was the lowest number I could get.
jokes.setup: Another barfly bragging about Edna
jokes.punchline: The implication that 171 other men had the same email idea
jokes.explanation: Clever escalation of Edna's promiscuity through email address scarcity. Good specific detail.

jokes.text: And after a night with Edna, I couldn't walk this way, that way, or any other way.
jokes.setup: Aerosmith drummer talking about Edna
jokes.punchline: Pun on the band's song 'Walk This Way' with sexual implications
jokes.explanation: Good celebrity cameo joke with song reference. Works but relies on recognition of the reference.

jokes.text: So Joey, is Wikipedia accurate when it says 'Walk This Way' was inspired by Young Frankenstein?
jokes.setup: Awkward transition after sexual revelation
jokes.punchline: Homer's oblivious shift to trivia questions
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer obliviousness to social cues. The Wikipedia reference is appropriately random.

jokes.text: Don't you mean 'neighboreeno'? / No. Just plain neighbor.
jokes.setup: Homer using Ned's typical friendly nickname
jokes.punchline: Ned's cold rejection of the familiar term
jokes.explanation: Good callback showing the deterioration of their relationship. The contrast is effective.

jokes.text: What does sound like a lot to you? What exactly are we talking about here?
jokes.setup: Ned's nervous rambling about staples
jokes.punchline: Edna's confusion about the actual topic
jokes.explanation: Standard nervous conversation comedy. Works but not particularly memorable.

jokes.text: That's right. Kiss all the men that have ever made their way to Edna's adventurous tongue.
jokes.setup: Edna asking for a kiss goodnight
jokes.punchline: Ned's bitter internal monologue about her past
jokes.explanation: Dark turn for Ned's character. The phrase 'adventurous tongue' is both bitter and creative.

jokes.text: Edna, call me Delta Airlines 'cause I can't handle your extra baggage.
jokes.setup: Ned breaking up with Edna
jokes.punchline: Airline metaphor for relationship problems
jokes.explanation: Clever airline metaphor for breakup. Good structure but slightly out of character for Ned's usual speech patterns.

jokes.text: What? My doctor said 'don't walk.' / That was a traffic signal.
jokes.setup: Marge suggesting Homer go on a walk
jokes.punchline: Homer's misinterpretation of pedestrian signals as medical advice
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer logic failure. The misdirection from medical to traffic context is well-executed.

jokes.text: And didn't the Easter Bunny himself say 'Forgive them, father, for finding all my eggs'?
jokes.setup: Homer trying to quote religious wisdom
jokes.punchline: Mixing Jesus's crucifixion words with Easter Bunny mythology
jokes.explanation: Brilliant religious/secular mashup. Homer's confused theology is perfectly in character and very quotable.

jokes.text: And if giant rabbit can lay chocolate eggs, maybe you and Edna can find a way to make it work.
jokes.setup: Homer continuing his Easter Bunny logic
jokes.punchline: Using impossible Easter mythology as relationship advice
jokes.explanation: Good follow-up to the Easter Bunny joke. The logic chain is appropriately ridiculous.

jokes.text: I still wear your bra. / Well, not exactly proud, but I'm stuck.
jokes.setup: Edna saying she's proud of her past relationships
jokes.punchline: Immediate contradiction and practical admission
jokes.explanation: Good character honesty moment. The immediate backtrack from pride to resignation works.

jokes.text: So we're going to let the Internet decide. Should Ned and Edna stay together? Visit thesimpsons.com and vote.
jokes.setup: Need to resolve relationship conflict
jokes.punchline: Breaking fourth wall to crowdsource the decision
jokes.explanation: Innovative fourth-wall break that was genuinely interactive. Character integration suffers but concept is creative.

jokes.text: all designed to distract you while we take your personal information and use it to apply for credit cards in your name.
jokes.setup: Listing voting options for the website
jokes.punchline: Revealing the true purpose of data collection
jokes.explanation: Smart satire of online data collection. The casual admission of fraud is darkly funny.

jokes.text: Also, if you wanna find the real shooter of Mr. Burns, go back and look for more clues. They're all there. / Homie, it was Maggie. / Yeah, right. A baby shot a guy.
jokes.setup: Homer suggesting viewers reexamine old episodes
jokes.punchline: His disbelief of the actual resolution to the show's mystery
jokes.explanation: Good callback to classic episodes. Homer's skepticism of his own show's plot is appropriately meta.

tmdb_overview: When Ned starts dating Mrs. Krabappel, he is surprised to learn how many of Springfield's men she has been with.
